name: Haikou
intro: Haikou suits travelers who want Hainan to feel like more than a resort handoff, especially when the route needs one old-port city layer, one gentler arrival, or a broader north-to-south island rhythm.
seoTitle: Haikou Travel Guide for First-Time Visitors
seoDescription: Plan a first trip to Haikou with practical advice on whether the city deserves a real stop, how long to stay, and when north Hainan improves the island route.
tripPace: Best when the stay stays compact and purposeful: one old-city block, one food-or-evening layer, and a clear reason for not going straight south.
highlights: A stronger answer than many travelers expect when the island needs city texture, humidity, trade memory, and a slower north-Hainan opening.
Works best when Qilou, food, and old-port atmosphere are read as a real chapter rather than as filler before Sanya.
Usually earns its place through mood and route logic, not through a blockbuster attraction count.

planningPriorities.title: Be honest about whether Haikou is a real stop
planningPriorities.description: If the trip only wants beach recovery, Haikou can become extra movement. If the island should feel broader, it can be exactly the right opening note.

planningPriorities.title: Build around Qilou and city texture, not headline attraction fantasy
planningPriorities.description: Haikou is strongest as atmosphere, food, arcade streets, and a different pace from the south coast.

planningPriorities.title: Use Haikou to improve rhythm, not just airport convenience
planningPriorities.description: The city works when it reduces exhaustion or adds character, not when it exists only because the flight happened to land there.

transportNote: Haikou becomes much easier once you decide whether the city is only the island's arrival point or a real north-Hainan chapter before moving south.
heroImageAlt: Historic arcaded street architecture in Haikou's Qilou district under bright subtropical daylight.
